name: BATTLESCAR: Punk Was Invented By Girls

reviews.review_description: The story is too artsy for my taste but I very much like how the story is being brought to life through the visuals.
I enjoy the visual story telling but i cannot enjoy it without subtitles being deaf….

As a Gen-X guy who grew up on 70s Punk, Battlescar is a compelling, authentic tale that captures what it was like during that era.  What's on offer is an approximately 30-minute story, rendered (beautifully) in VR with 3D models--it's a lot more compelling than it probably sounds.  The artists did an exceptional job, including small details many folks won't notice but lend authenticity.  For example, early in the story there's a tiny poster for the grindhouse film "Amin" which played on 42nd Street for years.  This is adult-only fare as it contains copious depictions of heroin, cocaine, glue sniffing, and violence.  Kudos to everyone who worked on this because everything from the voice performance to the animation, to the music/sound-design is executed perfectly.  The film is non-interactive and can be experienced seated or standing. If the price seems fair to you for an extremely memorable 30 minute film then absolutely grab it--I'm glad I did.

Wow!! Was not expecting such a deep and powerfully dark story. This one is for adults only and unlike any immersive animated film I've seen in VR. Very edgy with a lot of style!! Loved it!!! The devs of this short film should be super proud because they've created a masterpiece. We'll done!! 🔥🔥❤️❤️✌️
Deep story. Decent visuals. Nothing mind blowing but worth a watch!

Through the magic of VR, an average story becomes interesting.
The characters are all voiced by one narrator, she does a stellar job!

The only downside is that the three chapters are all short. Short and sweet. Perhaps better than a long and less impactful tale would have been? 
The art style, models and effects are impressive. It is a shame they didn’t also create an interactive game using the same assets.

All in all, if you like Punk, this is a must own title.
Interresting use of VR with cool use of perspective and cuts. The story is adult oriented, not suitable for kids I would say.

It amazes me on how this incredible Story selling so cheap … this is my favourite one so far . I was once a Punk myself … I wished they d do more like it … if s also visually stunning… I love it soooo much .

reviews.review_title: Loved the artistic direction and VR narr
reviews.review_description:
The film’s art direction reinvent itself every few moments, moving from trainset dioramas and animated comic strips to first-person views and hand-scrawled animations in the blink of an eye. 

Only thing that could have been better is the Voice over interpretation.

Great job !
I enjoy watching vr movies and this one is awesome i love the graphics the only complaint i have is i had to turn my head a little bit too far for comfort but other than that its perfect

reviews.review_title: Underground VR storytelling at its best
reviews.review_description: Very smart way to use VR to explore an underground environment, exploring oniric transitions and playing with scales to explore story techniques in an inventive and meaningful way. It’ll be good to see more studios and projects like this pioneering storytelling techniques through VR. Simply Very Good. Compliments to the creatives.
It sucked

reviews.review_title: Great story and use of the medium
reviews.review_description: Brilliant animated storytelling with imaginative use of the medium. Really impressed. I urge everyone to support this I don’t think you’ll regret the purchase.

Now this is a story. I loved it from start to finish. This is what VR was made for. I felt like I was there with the characters. The Devs really did a great job and I’d buy future content from them in a heartbeat if it comes close to or better than this.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback, we are very happy you enjoyed this experience!

reviews.review_description:
This is a wonderfully imaginative and entertaining retelling of the early days of punk in NYC. I love the way the different animation techniques are used in the storytelling - really inventive, hats off to the creators. 
I wish Oculus had a separate category for these types of non game immersive artistic story experiences, it would make it a whole lot easier to navigate and find content

reviews.review_title: Too short but amazing and compelling
reviews.review_description: This is what I want from a narrative VR game or in this case “experience” it’s short enough to watch all 3 parts in one sitting but long enough to get a real feel for the characters and situations. Got confused at one point about “No Singles” referring to cash and not one person on their own. Maybe that’s because I’m from Britain or perhaps it’s just me being thick. Worth your time though. More please.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback, we are very happy you enjoyed this experience!

reviews.review_description: This was such an awesome use of the VR platform! The creative way the Devs mixed fast paced editing with engaging scenes and characters was so cool. I loved how each scene drew you in...and in a completely different way. In one chapter you’re riding on the back of a bike suddenly and I could almost feel the streets flying by! This is a great example of using the platform in a unique way. I hope more Devs make content like this. I do have to mention that it is not for kids though...don’t let the animation fool you. There is definitely mature content in this one. But give it a shot and support creative VR media!

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback, we are very happy you enjoyed this experience!

reviews.review_description: 30-ish minutes of great storytelling. Good music, great graphics, and great voice acting set this apart.  There must be 30 different ways they lay out the scenes in this.  This is what VR storytelling could be.
Amazing storytelling, I would love to see more stuff like this.
This animated experience had great voice acting, sound design, art design, and an adult story line. The mix of artistic styles and perspectives made each scene unique.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback, we are very happy you enjoyed this experience!

reviews.review_description: This was overwhelmingly beautiful. You are immersed in art.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback, we are very happy you enjoyed this experience!

reviews.review_description: At first I was underwhelmed by the lack of controls. But when I realized, I was really amazed by the experience! It's limited by the Quest Hardware, but it's still incredible!

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback, we are very happy you enjoyed this experience!

reviews.review_description: Loved it,  Great Job.
More.

Very impressing to watch. The way it's been told, the visuals, the speed of it all.... It all fits! What a great way to show VR is  more than just games. Good job! Must see if you like VR movies.

Just one thing.... Think I might have seen a bug in the last scene. Lupa on the sofa doesn't move at all and is already floating mid air before the sofa appears....
I guess this is a small bug.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback, we are very happy you enjoyed this experience! 
We will share you feedback about the but in the last scene to the developers.

reviews.review_description: loved it. very well done....definitly worth it

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback, we are very happy you enjoyed this experience!

reviews.review_description: Amazing mix of techniques. Excellent storytelling
Great story and great visuals.
Fantastic punk rock allegory of kick-a$$ women told through the fullest capability of VR. Especially adored the conceptualisation of Elda throughout. 🤟

This is actually great experience. We need more works like this. It's something new and unique for me, which is cool. Story and narration are excellent, I liked visual metaphors and inventive design. I wonder when we'll start getting entire vr films. That would be something

reviews.review_title: Everything we knew VR films could be
reviews.review_description:
This is NOT your average VR short film. Battlescar tells a great story, complete with expert narration that draws you in, all while making use of stunning VR 3d visuals like I have never seen before in a VR film. 

This is a complete package of a short film experience that has never been done before with VR. 

The bar for VR animated films has officially been set. Just watch it.

Great use of VR graphics to tell a story right up until the abrupt and pointless ending. It’s too bad this wasn’t about overcoming adversity and achieving something but this is what goes for story telling these days. A lot of sizzle but no steak. Worth it for the brief VR experience but that’s it.
Just buy it, sit back and watch top entertainment. Good enough to win an Oscar for a short film imho. Great graphics, music and storytelling. Will def look out for more from these devs.
I loved this.  The story line from the 80’s punk scene &  CBGB’s.  The animation was incredible!🤩👍💕
I really recommend this production. It has energy it has a great social and individual wonderful message. Love this visuals, the music and the story. Congrats!! 🙌💕🇵🇷

I really liked this, the presentation was just right, the world felt real, dirty and dangerous. It was like watching a movie, but being in the room with the characters, who were all great.  If the Battle Scars make an album, I want the vinyl.

Giving it three stars, the artwork was good and can't fault the effort put into this.
However I really didn't enjoy the style used and the story was nothing to write about.
In fact I kept looking to see how long was  left, because it was only 45 mins long that I watched it all, longer would have been off.
Can understand people liking it, but aound a fiver for 45mins and to me boring(could be my age lol) left a nasty taste that I won't try anymore.

PS I do understand that these things are niche and only has a small audience and not complaining about spending a fiver for something, but when it does nothing for you, after all the reviews I read with positives might have heightened my expectations and failed to deliver.

Bought this as it said no Internet connection required, im currently offline due to my phones hotspot being unable to connect back to my q2 headset..
At start it says qued, will start when a wifi connection is available?
its been 3weeks since my connection stopped.
I'd bought my q2 6weeks ago,ive no wifi and was able to use my phones hotspot for 3weeks,even to set the headset up i used hotspot.
Any help to connect would be appreciated.

My first vr experience, was dope cool story , wish it was longer
